User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the ChoripanFlow application, such as a cashier, administrator, or kitchen staff. This entity stores profile information and links the user to their assigned branch and role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication and identification.

role.description: The role of the user within the application (e.g., 'Admin', 'Cashier', 'KitchenStaff'), determining their access and permissions.

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch where the user is primarily assigned. (Relationship: Branch 1:N User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Branch.title: Branch
Branch.description: Represents a physical location or store of the ChoripanFlow business. Each branch has its own menu, orders, and associated users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Branch entity.

name.description: The official name of the branch.

address.description: The physical address of the branch location.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number for the branch.

taxRate.description: The applicable tax rate for sales at this branch (e.g., 0.08 for 8%).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the branch record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the branch record was last updated.

MenuCategory.title: MenuCategory
MenuCategory.description: Organizes menu items into logical groups (e.g., 'Appetizers', 'Main Courses', 'Drinks') for easier navigation and management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the menu category (e.g., 'Sandwiches').

description.description: A brief description of what this category entails.

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch to which this menu category belongs. (Relationship: Branch 1:N MenuCategory)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu category was last updated.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single item available on the menu, including its price, description, and category.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item (e.g., 'Classic Choripan').

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item, including ingredients or customization options.

price.description: The standard selling price of the menu item.

imageUrl.description: An optional URL to an image of the menu item.

categoryId.description: Reference to the MenuCategory this item belongs to. (Relationship: MenuCategory 1:N MenuItem)

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch where this menu item is available. (Relationship: Branch 1:N MenuItem)

isAvailable.description: Indicates whether the menu item is currently available for purchase.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase transaction, tracking its status, totals, and associated payments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

orderNumber.description: A human-readable identifier for the order, unique within a branch.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'draft', 'confirmed', 'paid', 'cancelled', 'completed').

subtotal.description: The sum of all item prices in the order before tax.

taxAmount.description: The calculated tax amount for the order.

totalAmount.description: The grand total amount due for the order (subtotal + tax).

amountPaid.description: The cumulative amount paid towards this order through various payments.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was initially created.

confirmedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the order was confirmed and sent to the kitchen.

completedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the order was fully paid and completed.

cashierId.description: Reference to the User (cashier) who created or last modified this order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch where this order was placed. (Relationship: Branch 1:N Order)

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific menu item included in an order, capturing its quantity and price at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem that was purchased. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

itemName.description: A snapshot of the menu item's name at the time of order, for historical accuracy.

quantity.description: The number of units of this menu item purchased.

unitPrice.description: The price of one unit of the item at the time the order was confirmed, locked to prevent changes.

totalPrice.description: The total price for this specific order item (quantity * unitPrice).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order item was last updated.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records a single payment made towards an order, including the amount, method, and processing cashier.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this payment is for. (Relationship: Order 1:N Payment)

amount.description: The amount of money processed in this payment.

method.description: The payment method used (e.g., 'Cash', 'Credit Card', 'Debit Card', 'Mobile Pay').

paymentDate.description: Timestamp when the payment was recorded.

cashierId.description: Reference to the User (cashier) who processed this payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was last updated.

KitchenTicket.title: KitchenTicket
KitchenTicket.description: Represents a ticket generated for the kitchen staff, detailing the items to be prepared for a confirmed order and tracking their preparation status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the KitchenTicket entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order for which this kitchen ticket was generated. (Relationship: Order 1:N KitchenTicket)

status.description: The current preparation status of the kitchen ticket (e.g., 'New', 'Preparing', 'Ready', 'Served', 'Cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the kitchen ticket was created/generated.

startedPreparingAt.description: Optional timestamp when kitchen staff started preparing the items on this ticket.

readyAt.description: Optional timestamp when all items on this ticket were ready for pickup/serving.

servedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the items on this ticket were served to the customer.

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch where this kitchen ticket originated, for KDS filtering. (Relationship: Branch 1:N KitchenTicket)

cashierId.description: Reference to the User (cashier) who confirmed the order, useful for kitchen communication. (Relationship: User 1:N KitchenTicket)

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The 'id' field matches 'request.auth.uid'. Includes primary 'role' and 'branchId' which are the source of truth, but denormalized into 'BranchUserRole' documents for efficient authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Top-level collection for each physical business branch, storing its details.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier for the branch.

definition.description: Auxiliary collection for robust, authorization-independent role checks. Each document in this subcollection indicates a 'User's 'role' within a specific 'Branch'. This structure enables efficient 'exists()' checks in security rules, avoiding 'get()' calls on the root '/users' collection. This document only needs to store the 'role' field (e.g., { role: 'Admin' }). This must be kept in sync with the primary User document.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the branch.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with this role in the branch.

definition.description: Categories for menu items, specific to each branch. Includes denormalized 'branchId' for efficient access and authorization independence.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the branch.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu category.

definition.description: Individual menu items available at a specific branch. Includes denormalized 'branchId' and 'categoryId' for efficient access and authorization independence.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the branch.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item.

definition.description: Customer orders placed at a specific branch. Includes denormalized 'branchId' and 'cashierId' for robust authorization and query capabilities.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the branch where the order was placed.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Individual items within an 'Order'. For authorization independence, the schema **must be extended** to include 'branchId' and 'cashierId' in addition to existing 'orderId' and 'menuItemId'. These denormalized fields enable direct rule checks without parent document lookups.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the branch related to the order (denormalized).

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item.

definition.description: Records individual payments made against an 'Order'. For authorization independence, the schema **must be extended** to include 'branchId' in addition to existing 'orderId' and 'cashierId'. This denormalized field enables direct rule checks without parent document lookups.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the branch related to the order (denormalized).

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the payment.

definition.description: Tickets for kitchen staff detailing items to prepare for an 'Order'. Includes denormalized 'branchId', 'orderId', and 'cashierId' for efficient KDS filtering and authorization.

params.name: branchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the branch where the kitchen ticket originated.

params.name: ticketId
params.description: The unique identifier of the kitchen ticket.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for ChoripanFlow is designed to maximize security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This is achieved primarily through two mechanisms:
1.  **Denormalized `branchId` and `cashierId`:** Every document in subcollections under `/branches/{branchId}` (e.g., `menuCategories`, `menuItems`, `orders`, `kitchenTickets`, `orderItems`, `payments`) explicitly stores its associated `branchId`. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent `Branch` documents when authorizing access to subcollection data. For `OrderItem` and `Payment` entities, their schemas **must be extended** to include `branchId` (and `cashierId` for `OrderItem`) to ensure this independence. The `cashierId` is also denormalized into `Order` and `KitchenTicket` documents for similar reasons.
2.  **`user_roles` Subcollection:** To avoid `get()` calls on the global `/users/{userId}` collection for determining a user's branch and role context, a dedicated auxiliary collection `user_roles` is introduced under each branch: `/branches/{branchId}/user_roles/{userId}`. A document in this collection signifies that `userId` is authorized for `branchId` and contains their `role`. This enables efficient `exists()` checks directly on the relevant branch-specific authorization context in security rules.

**Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The hierarchical path structure (`/branches/{branchId}/...`) clearly delineates ownership and relationships. Denormalized fields such as `branchId` and `cashierId` explicitly state the authorization context within each document, making security rules simpler and more transparent. Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{branchId}`, `{orderId}`).

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles and branch assignments are stored in the database. The `User` document (`/users/{userId}`) acts as the source of truth for a user's primary `branchId` and `role`. However, for *rule evaluation*, the denormalized `user_roles` collection (`/branches/{branchId}/user_roles/{userId}`) is used for `exists()` checks and `get().data.role` lookups, removing the dependency on `request.auth.token.claims`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
-   The structural segregation of all transactional and menu data under `/branches/{branchId}`, combined with the `user_roles` subcollection, directly supports QAPs. A user attempting to `list` documents in any branch-specific collection (e.g., `/branches/{branchId}/menuItems`) can only do so if their `request.auth.uid` corresponds to an `exists()` check in `/branches/{branchId}/user_roles/$(request.auth.uid)`. This ensures that `list` queries are inherently secure and do not return unauthorized data.
-   Further, specific role checks (`get(/branches/$(branchId)/user_roles/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'Admin'`) can be used to restrict write/update operations for different entity types, ensuring rules are not used as filters for write operations.

**Invariants:** The structure supports invariants by consistently storing ownership (`cashierId`), timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`), and explicitly managing denormalized data to maintain consistency across related documents through atomic writes or Cloud Functions triggers. Explicit `status` fields (`Order.status`, `KitchenTicket.status`) ensure predictable state management.

**Onboarding and Branch Configuration:** Upon user registration and subsequent onboarding, a new `Branch` document is created in `/branches/{newBranchId}`. The user's `/users/{userId}` document is updated with `newBranchId` and `role: "Admin"`. Crucially, a corresponding document is then created in `/branches/{newBranchId}/user_roles/{userId}` with `role: "Admin"`. This ensures the newly registered admin has immediate and secure access to configure their new branch, satisfying the user's request.
